concerns.text: Data Provenance & License Gap: Section 4.1 (Document Pool) states the pool contains 1.5M PDFs from 'multiple sources' but omits specific dataset names, licenses, or access URLs. Section 4.2 mentions 'Seed 2.0' for QA generation but lacks a citation or license check for the generator model. Without explicit provenance (e.g., 'derived from arXiv PDFs under CC-BY' or 'proprietary internal logs'), the reproducibility of the data synthesis pipeline is unverifiable.

Missing Data Versioning: Section 4.1 mentions filtering benchmarks using SHA-256 hashes but does not specify the version of the benchmark datasets (e.g., MMLongBench v1.1) used for the overlap check. Section 7 (Appendix) lists 'manifest.json' in the plan but the paper text does not reference a specific manifest ID or checksum for the 1.5M document pool, making the exact data snapshot unreproducible.

External Link Rot Risk: Section 4.1 includes a footnote for PyMuPDF (github.com/pymupdf/pymupdf) but lacks a specific commit hash or release tag. If the repository changes or the link rotates, the exact rendering parameters (DPI=144) used to generate the 128K token sequences cannot be verified. A specific version tag is required for data quality assurance.
